programs.name: PASSPORT
programs.what_it_does: PASSPORT is Ohio's Medicaid home and community-based services waiver program that provides long-term care services to allow seniors and disabled individuals to remain in their homes rather than nursing facilities. It covers personal care, homemaker services, adult day care, and other supports to promote independence.[3]
programs.who_its_for: Ohio residents aged 60+ or disabled individuals assessed as needing nursing home level of care, meeting Medicaid financial eligibility.
programs.benefit_value: Varies by assessed needs; annual waiver slots limited, covers services up to nursing home cost equivalent.

programs.name: Ohio Medicaid
programs.what_it_does: Ohio Medicaid provides health coverage for low-income seniors and disabled, including long-term care for those 65+ or disabled. For seniors, it covers nursing home care, home health, and pairs with Medicare for dual eligibles.[1][3]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income Ohio seniors 65+, disabled adults meeting income/asset limits (e.g., under 300% SSI for aged/disabled).
programs.benefit_value: Full health coverage; no premium for qualified, copays minimal.

programs.name: PACE (Program of All-Inclusive Care for the Elderly)
programs.what_it_does: PACE provides comprehensive medical and social services to frail seniors in community settings, including adult day health, transportation, and home care to avoid nursing homes. In Ohio, administered via select providers under Medicaid/Medicare.[3]
programs.who_its_for: Ohio seniors 55+ certified for nursing home level care, able to live safely in community, Medicaid eligible.
programs.benefit_value: All-inclusive capitation; covers all Medicare/Medicaid services plus extras, premium ~$0 for qualified.

programs.name: Medicare Savings Programs (QMB, SLMB, QI)
programs.what_it_does: These programs pay Medicare premiums, deductibles, and copays for low-income beneficiaries; QMB covers Part A/B full, SLMB/QI cover Part B. In Ohio, called Medicare Savings Program via Medicaid.[5]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income Ohio Medicare beneficiaries; QMB ≤100% FPL, SLMB 100-120%, QI 120-135% FPL (2026 figures approx. $1,255-$1,690/mo single).
programs.benefit_value: QMB: $174.70 Part B + deductibles/copays; SLMB/QI: Part B premium only ($174.70/mo 2024).

programs.name: Ohio SNAP (Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program)
programs.what_it_does: Provides monthly EBT card for food purchases to low-income households. Ohio administers federally via county DJFS.[4]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income Ohio seniors 60+ with income ≤200% FPL (higher deductions for elderly), assets limited.
programs.benefit_value: Avg $200-300/mo per senior household; max $291 single (2026 adjusted).

programs.name: Home Energy Assistance Program (HEAP)
programs.what_it_does: HEAP provides grants to pay heating bills and crisis assistance for shut-off prevention. Ohio winter program federally funded.[4]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income Ohio households ≤175% FPL, priority seniors/disabled on SSI/Medicaid.
programs.benefit_value: Avg $400-700 annual benefit per household; crisis up to $1,000.

programs.name: Weatherization Assistance Program (WAP)
programs.what_it_does: WAP funds home energy efficiency upgrades like insulation, sealing, furnace repair for low-income homes. Ohio DOE-administered federally.[3]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income Ohio households ≤200% FPL, priority seniors 60+, disabled.
programs.benefit_value: Free services avg $6,500 value per home in upgrades.

programs.name: Ohio Senior Health Insurance Information Program (OSHIIP/SHIP)
programs.what_it_does: Provides free counseling on Medicare, supplements, savings programs, and appeals. Ohio's SHIP via Dept of Insurance/ODA.[5]
programs.who_its_for: All Ohio Medicare beneficiaries 65+, caregivers; no income limit.
programs.benefit_value: Free one-on-one counseling; saves avg $500+/yr via plan optimization.

programs.name: Meals on Wheels (Home-Delivered Meals)
programs.what_it_does: Delivers nutritious meals to homebound seniors via OAA funding through local agencies. Ohio ODA oversees Area Agencies on Aging providers.[3]
programs.who_its_for: Homebound Ohio seniors 60+ unable to prepare meals, assessed need.

programs.name: National Family Caregiver Support Program (NFCSP)
programs.what_it_does: Offers respite care, training, counseling for caregivers of seniors. Ohio via ODA/Area Agencies.[3]
programs.who_its_for: Unpaid family caregivers of Ohioans 60+ or 55+ special needs.
programs.benefit_value: Respite up to $32/hr or vouchers; varies by agency funding.

programs.name: Senior Community Service Employment Program (SCSEP)
programs.what_it_does: Provides part-time paid training/work at min wage in community nonprofits for low-income seniors. Ohio via AARP/Experience Works etc.[3]
programs.benefit_value: 20 hrs/wk at $7.25-$15/hr, leads to unsubsidized jobs.

programs.name: Legal Assistance Developer Program (LADP)
programs.what_it_does: Funds legal aid for low-income seniors on elder rights, benefits, abuse via OAA. Ohio ODA coordinates Area Agencies.[3]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income Ohio seniors 60+ needing civil legal help.

programs.name: Long-Term Care Ombudsman Program
programs.what_it_dos: Advocates for residents in nursing homes, assisted living; investigates complaints, ensures rights. Ohio via ODA/Regional Ombudsmen.[3]
programs.who_its_for: Residents/family in Ohio long-term care facilities.

programs.name: Golden Buckeye Program
programs.what_it_does: The Golden Buckeye Program connects older Ohio residents with businesses offering discounts, waived fees, free products, and more to help save money. Ohio residents are automatically enrolled at age 60 and receive a card in the mail.[1]
programs.who_its_for: Ohio residents aged 60 and older, or those aged 18-59 with a disability.[1]
programs.whats_unique: Automatic enrollment at 60 with a state-issued card providing statewide discounts not available as a universal federal program.[1]
programs.benefit_value: Discounts, waived fees, and free products from participating businesses; value varies by usage.

programs.name: Healthy Aging Grants
programs.what_it_does: This program provides $40 million in grants to 85 Ohio counties to support local aging services helping adults 60+ stay healthy, live longer, and maintain independence. Funds bolster food/housing assistance, internet access, and digital literacy.[2][7]
programs.who_its_for: Ohioans aged 60 and older, distributed through county programs.[2]
programs.whats_unique: State-funded initiative established via Ohio's 2023 budget (House Bill 33), offering flexible local grants with base $100,000 per county plus extras based on non-Medicaid seniors 60+.[2]
programs.benefit_value: $40 million total statewide; counties get $100,000 base + additional based on senior population.

programs.name: Senior Citizen Tax Credit
programs.what_it_does: This provides a tax credit on Ohio income tax for qualifying retirement and pension income. The maximum benefit is capped at $200 for eligible seniors.[3]
programs.who_its_for: Ohio residents aged 65+ with modified adjusted gross income below $100,000.[3]
programs.whats_unique: Ohio-specific state income tax credit targeting senior retirement/pension income, beyond federal standards.[3]

programs.name: Home Repair Program
programs.what_it_does: This state-funded program offers minor home repairs and modifications for eligible older adults. It supports safe aging in place through the Ohio Department of Aging.[5]
programs.who_its_for: Ohio adults aged 60+ meeting eligibility criteria.[5]
programs.whats_unique: Ohio Department of Aging-funded initiative for home modifications, not a standard federal offering.[5]
programs.benefit_value: Minor repairs and accessibility modifications; specific value depends on approval.

programs.name: Franklin County Senior Services Levy Programs (Senior Options)
programs.what_it_does: Senior Options connects Franklin County seniors to services like adult day care, respite care, case management, meals, and homemaker services to maintain independence. It serves as a one-stop access point funded by a local property tax levy.[6]
programs.whats_unique: Funded by Franklin County's Senior Services Levy (voter-approved since 1992), providing county-specific property tax-supported senior options beyond federal programs.[6]
programs.benefit_value: Access to multiple services (meals, care, transport); value varies by enrollment.
